scene.scenePrompt: Morning sunlight streams through tall windows, casting long shadows across a dusty tea table with scattered cups and saucers. Close-up on Henry Graves, seated at the head, his face composed, eyes directly meeting the viewer with a calm, knowing gaze, lips slightly curved. Behind him, out of focus but visible: Eleanor Blackwood with hands over her mouth, eyes wide; William Harper standing with cane, leaning forward, sharp eyes scanning; Margaret Spencer’s body slumped on the floor, still. Warm light contrasts with the chilling stillness. Cozy mystery atmosphere, tension hanging in the air.

beats.narration: Sunlight streams through the glass window onto the long table, the aroma of tea still lingering. The laughter stops abruptly—the finely dressed noblewoman's pupils dilate, and her body slowly slides down, collapsing to the floor. Everyone freezes in place.
beats.speaker: Henry Graves
beats.line: Now, everyone, please remain calm. The killer is among us.
beats.lineDelivery: Voice calm and steady, speaking slowly with a hint of unquestionable authority, eyes scanning every face

activeCharacters.name: Henry Graves
activeCharacters.pose: Sitting upright in the host's seat, holding a teacup, calmly scanning the crowd with a barely perceptible smile at the corner of his mouth

activeCharacters.name: Eleanor Blackwood
activeCharacters.pose: Sitting next to the deceased, covering her mouth with both hands, eyes wide with terror

activeCharacters.name: William Harper
activeCharacters.pose: Standing aside, leaning on his cane, body slightly bent forward, scanning the scene with sharp eyes

activeCharacters.name: Margaret Spencer
activeCharacters.pose: Body slumped on the floor, facial expression frozen, eyes wide open, showing no signs of life

choices.label: Crouch down to examine the body

beats.narration: Suppressing your racing heartbeat, you look around. Eleanor is pale, her hands trembling; Detective William stands like a statue, motionless. Out of the corner of your eye, you catch the corner of the deceased's mouth—a trace of white powder, and her fingernails are tinged with an ominous purplish-blue.

activeCharacters.name: Henry Graves
activeCharacters.pose: Still holding his teacup, his gaze following you, showing a look of approval

activeCharacters.name: Eleanor Blackwood
activeCharacters.pose: Eyes dodging, hands wringing her handkerchief tightly

activeCharacters.name: William Harper
activeCharacters.pose: Leaning on his cane, nodding slightly, seemingly observing you as well

activeCharacters.name: Margaret Spencer
activeCharacters.pose: The body lies sprawled, the powder at the corner of her mouth clearly visible

beats.narration: You crouch down, suppressing your discomfort to get closer to the body. There is indeed white powder at the corner of the deceased's mouth, and her fingernails are purplish-blue—a classic reaction to poison. Just as you are about to examine further, you are interrupted by a soft cough.

activeCharacters.name: Henry Graves
activeCharacters.pose: Frowning slightly, but not stopping you

activeCharacters.name: Eleanor Blackwood
activeCharacters.pose: Screaming and taking two steps back

activeCharacters.name: William Harper
activeCharacters.pose: Stepping closer, tapping his cane lightly on the floor

activeCharacters.name: Margaret Spencer
activeCharacters.pose: The body lies sprawled, powder at the corner of her mouth, fingernails purplish-blue

beats.narration: Henry puts down his teacup, stands up, and makes a gesturing motion of 'after you' towards you.
beats.speaker: Henry Graves
beats.line: Young man, you have a good pair of eyes. Come with me to the study, I have something to say to you.
beats.lineDelivery: Tone gentle but firm, eyes deep and searching

activeCharacters.name: Henry Graves
activeCharacters.pose: Standing up, adjusting his vestments, gesturing for you to follow

choices.label: Follow the priest to the study

effect.nextSceneSeed: The priest's study, flames dancing in the fireplace; he closes the door, his expression turning serious

beats.narration: William Harper gently blocks your hand with his cane, tilting his head toward the door.
beats.speaker: William Harper
beats.line: Young man, don't disturb the scene. If you want to know the truth, take a walk with me in the garden.
beats.lineDelivery: Lowering his voice, with seasoned caution, gesturing with his eyes for you to cooperate

activeCharacters.name: William Harper
activeCharacters.pose: Turning slightly, pointing his cane toward the door, with a mysterious smile on his face

choices.label: Go to the garden with William

effect.nextSceneSeed: A stone path in the garden, William walking ahead leaning on his cane, a cold wind sweeping through the hedges

characters.visualDescription: female, 30-35 years old, elegant middle-aged woman, dark auburn hair styled in a soft chignon, few silver streaks, warm hazel eyes with a hint of knowing intelligence, fair complexion with subtle laugh lines, signature attire: a deep burgundy wool dress with delicate lace collar and cameo brooch, matching velvet cardigan with embroidered floral pattern, pearl earrings, overall aura of refined maturity, gentle but observant, cozy mystery style, warm candlelit tones

storyState.logline: A deadly tea party, a noblewoman dying of laughter, and a priest calmly sipping tea. You must find the killer among a group of friendly neighbors, or will you be the next one to die laughing?
storyState.genreTags: Suspense / Mystery / Twist-filled Drama / English Countryside
storyState.protagonist: You are a young amateur detective who has just arrived from London for a vacation in this peaceful village. This morning, you were invited by the priest to a tea party. While everyone was chatting and laughing, a noblewoman suddenly burst into uncontrollable laughter, collapsed, and died. Yet the priest calmly sipped his black tea, as if everything was expected. You realize the killer is among this group of seemingly friendly neighbors, and you must uncover the truth before tonight—because someone wants you to be the next laughing stock of this deadly tea party.
storyState.castNotes:
Henry Graves: The priest. Outwardly gentle and benevolent, but actually sees through everything. He hosts the tea party but remains unusually calm in the face of death. Having met you only recently, he seems to know something.
Eleanor Blackwood: The niece of the deceased. Young and beautiful, a suspect to inherit the estate. She speaks evasively and harbors an inexplicable hostility toward you.
William Harper: A retired detective. Leaning on a cane, elegant in manner, always observing others. He could be a helper or a red herring.
storyState.synopsis: At the morning tea party, a noblewoman suddenly laughs and collapses dead. The priest calmly points out that the killer is among them. You notice white powder at the corner of the deceased's mouth and purplish-blue fingernails, suggesting poisoning. You choose to either observe the crowd or examine the body, and are subsequently invited for a private talk by either the priest or the retired detective. The truth is about to be revealed.
openThreads: Was the noblewoman's cause of death poisoning? How did the poison get into the tea treats?
Why is the priest so calm? Did he know beforehand?
Is Eleanor's fear out of grief or a guilty conscience?
Why did Detective William ask to speak with you privately? What clues does he hold?
relationships: Henry Graves: Expresses approval of your observation skills, seemingly intending to guide you
Eleanor Blackwood: Looks terrified, behaving suspiciously
William Harper: Approaches you proactively, hinting that he knows the inside story
storyState.nextHook: You are about to learn key inside information about this tea party in a private conversation, but the truth may be more dangerous than you think.

worldSetting:
This is an AI interactive story game oriented towards a 【Male-oriented】 audience, featuring intense dramatic conflicts and rapid twists in the style of short videos.
Story Style: Multi-line twists. Pace: Fast and satisfying.
Opening setting of the featured story 'The Priest's Tea Party': In a peaceful English village, the priest hosts a weekly tea party. This morning, a noblewoman died laughing at the tea party. Sipping his black tea, the priest looks at the subtle expressions of everyone present; he knows the killer is among these seemingly friendly neighbors.
Please start directly with this opening to give the player a strong sense of immersion and instant gratification. Subsequent branches should maintain the twist density of a short drama, allowing players to see immediate consequences for every choice they make.
styleGuide: Cozy mystery illustration, vertical composition. A handsome young amateur sleuth in a tweed coat, looking through a magnifying glass inside a warm, candlelit village library at night, snow falling outside the window. Comforting, mysterious mood.
